hero.headline: Madison Arndt
body: I’m a developer who builds modular, functional systems designed to be both independent and scalable. I focus on creating tools and projects that can stand on their own while also supporting larger ecosystems.
Outside of coding, I stay active in the outdoors: walking, biking, hiking, and kayaking and I run a free outdoor exercise group in Anoka County and surrounding areas.

about.title: About Me
body: I’m a developer with a strong focus on modular design and adaptability. I build projects in structured components so they can be reused, expanded, or integrated without unnecessary complexity. My approach keeps systems lightweight, maintainable, and practical for real-world use.

One of the ways I stand out is how I adapt my syntax and structure to match existing repositories and environments. Instead of forcing a single style, I prioritize consistency within each project, making collaboration and long-term maintenance easier.

Beyond development, I value staying active and connected. I regularly spend time outdoors and lead a free community exercise group, combining structure, consistency, and accessibility principles I also apply in my technical work.
I aim to build things that are useful, reliable, and easy to work with—whether for myself, a team, or a larger system.
